activation_results.reason: Input matches the canonical use case in the description verbatim.
Architect is a clear synonym for Design; rest of the phrase matches the description.
User explicitly invokes the skill by its name ga4-events.
SSH key forwarding is unrelated to GA4 or analytics event design.
Generic meta question about skills, not a GA4 event design request.

criterion_results.text: No American spellings present in the narrative (color, optimize, behavior, organize, center, defense, license as a verb, analyze) — Australian or neutral forms both pass
criterion_results.evidence: No US spellings detected; uses AU/neutral forms ('catalogue', 'AEST', 'AUD', dd/mm/yyyy dates, 'Afterpay').

criterion_results.text: Artefact includes both the design and its rationale (trade-offs surfaced)
criterion_results.evidence: Rationale columns on conversions and custom dimensions; explicit pushback on add_to_cart/begin_checkout with reasoning; Open issues surfaces further trade-offs.

criterion_results.text: Report includes the canonical sections: 'Event inventory (last 30 days)', 'Conversions'
criterion_results.evidence: Both headings present in the artefact (## Event inventory (last 30 days) and ## Conversions).

judge_result.notes: Artefact satisfies all three criteria. Spelling is consistently Australian/neutral with no flagged US forms. Design choices are paired with rationale, and the artefact explicitly surfaces trade-offs (declining add_to_cart/begin_checkout as conversions, deferring custom metrics, listing open issues). Both canonical section headings are present verbatim.
